title: Jeremiah, Chapter 45
description: Complete text of Jeremiah chapter 45 from the King James Version (1611).
introduction: Complete text of Jeremiah chapter 45 from the King James Version (1611).

translation.text:
<sup>1</sup>The word that Jeremiah the prophet spake unto Baruch the son of Neriah, when he had written these words in a book at the mouth of Jeremiah, in the fourth year of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah king of Judah, saying, <sup>2</sup>Thus saith the LORD, the God of Israel, unto thee, O Baruch; <sup>3</sup>Thou didst say, Woe is me now! for the LORD hath added grief to my sorrow; I fainted in my sighing, and I find no rest. <sup>4</sup>Thus shalt thou say unto him, The LORD saith thus; Behold,
that
 which I have built will I break down, and that which I have planted I will pluck up, even this whole land. <sup>5</sup>And seekest thou great things for thyself? seek
them not: for, behold, I will bring evil upon all flesh, saith the LORD: but thy life will I give unto thee for a prey in all places whither thou goest.
